title: Hut 8 Q3 2024: Pivot Sparks Revenue Growth, But Escalating Costs Loom
text: Hut 8 Corp exceeded analyst expectations with Q3 2024 revenue of $43.7 million versus the projected $35.1 million—nearly doubling the $21.7 million reported in Q3 2023. The growth reflects a significant strategic pivot away from traditional bitcoin mining. Key Revenue Drivers: Managed services generated $20.8 million (up from $4.8 million YoY). High-performance computing contributed $3.4 million (new contribution). Digital asset mining declined 26% to $11.6 million from $15.6 million YoY. Mining Challenges & Cost Escalation: Bitcoin production dropped dramatically with 234 BTC mined this quarter, down substantially from 675 BTC in the same period last year, representing a staggering 65% decrease. Mining costs per bitcoin reached $31,482, more than doubling from $15,020 a year earlier. Despite energy cost improvements (declining to $28.83/MWh from $42.73/MWh), total operational expenses rose to $17.6 million from $13.5 million, driven by expansion activities. Profitability Concerns: While net income improved to $0.9 million from a $4.4 million loss, adjusted EBITDA fell 51% to $5.6 million. Strategic Positioning: Hut 8 maintains 9,106 BTC ($576.5 million valuation) and $72.9 million in cash.
